perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination of the services to be performed by the undersigned (or earlier if requested by Goldman Sachs), the undersigned shall return to Goldman Sachs all copies of documents, papers or other material relating to Goldman Sachs or obtained or developed in the course of performing services for Goldman Sachs, or containing or derived from Confidential Information which are in the undersigned's possession, together, if requested by Goldman Sachs, with a certificate signed by the undersigned, in form and substance satisfactory to Goldman Sachs, to the effect that all such Confidential Information has been returned.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of the services to be performed by the undersigned (or earlier if requested by Goldman Sachs), the undersigned will make reasonable efforts to return to Goldman Sachs all copies of documents, papers or other material relating to Goldman Sachs or obtained or developed in the course of performing services for Goldman Sachs, or containing or derived from Confidential Information which are in the undersigned's possession, if reasonably possible, together, if requested by Goldman Sachs and if such request is deemed reasonable, with a certificate signed by the undersigned, in form and substance satisfactory to Goldman Sachs, to the effect that all such Confidential Information has been returned.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall return' to 'will make reasonable efforts to return' weakens the obligation to return confidential materials. The added condition 'if reasonably possible' provides an escape clause. Furthermore, the 'if requested by Goldman Sachs and if such request is deemed reasonable' adds another layer of ambiguity to the certificate requirement, allowing the undersigned to determine the reasonableness of the request and creating an In-Text contradiction by making the compliance uncertain with respect to I, the original obligation of full compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I. Non-Disclosure In connection with services now or in the future performed by the undersigned for Goldman, Sachs & Co. or for any subsidiary or affiliate of Goldman, Sachs & Co. (collectively called "Goldman Sachs"), the undersigned may have access to non-public information or materials describing or relating to Goldman Sachs or its clients, or third parties to whom Goldman Sachs has a duty of confidentiality, including materials describing or relating to the business affairs, policies or procedures of Goldman Sachs or its clients or such third parties; formulas; strategies; methods; processes; computer materials including source or object codes, data files, computer listings, computer programs, and other computer materials (regardless of the medium in which they are stored); or other information ("Confidential Information"). With respect to such Confidential Information, the undersigned acknowledges and agrees as follows: 1. The undersigned will endeavor to hold Confidential Information in reasonable confidence and will not, nor will it permit any agent, servant or employee to, copy, reproduce, sell, assign, license, market, transfer or otherwise dispose of, give or disclose Confidential Information to any direct competitor of Goldman Sachs, including any partner or employee of Goldman Sachs who does not have a need to know the Confidential Information. 2. Upon the termination of the services to be performed by the undersigned (or earlier if requested by Goldman Sachs), the undersigned will make reasonable efforts to return to Goldman Sachs all copies of documents, papers or other material relating to Goldman Sachs or obtained or developed in the course of performing services for Goldman Sachs, or containing or derived from Confidential Information which are in the undersigned's possession, if reasonably possible, together, if requested by Goldman Sachs and if such request is deemed reasonable, with a certificate signed by the undersigned, in form and substance satisfactory to Goldman Sachs, to the effect that all such Confidential Information has been returned.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the return of confidential information ('shall return'). The changed text weakens this obligation to 'will make reasonable efforts to return' and adds 'if reasonably possible', providing an escape clause. Furthermore, the 'if requested by Goldman Sachs and if such request is deemed reasonable' adds another layer of ambiguity to the certificate requirement. This contradicts the original obligation of full compliance as stated in paragraph I. Non-Disclosure which is also shown as the contradicted text, making compliance uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned agrees that the undersigned will not, without the prior written consent of Goldman Sachs in each instance: (a) use in advertising, publicity or otherwise the name of Goldman Sachs or any trade name, trademark, trade device, servicemark, symbol or any abbreviation, contraction or simulation thereof owned by Goldman Sachs; or (b) represent, directly or indirectly, that any product or any service provided by the undersigned has been approved or endorsed by Goldman Sachs.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned agrees that the undersigned will not, without the prior written consent of Goldman Sachs for significant public campaigns: (a) use in advertising, publicity or otherwise the name of Goldman Sachs or any trade name, trademark, trade device, servicemark, symbol or any abbreviation, contraction or simulation thereof owned by Goldman Sachs in a manner that could reasonably harm Goldman Sachs' reputation; or (b) represent, directly or indirectly, that any major product or any service provided by the undersigned has been explicitly approved or endorsed by Goldman Sachs.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes a 'significant public campaign' requiring consent, thus creating an in-text contradiction with III. The original strict prohibition on using Goldman Sachs' name or implying endorsement is relaxed and becomes subjective. The phrases 'in a manner that could reasonably harm Goldman Sachs' reputation' and 'explicitly approved or endorsed' introduce uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: II. Non-Promotion The undersigned agrees that the undersigned will not, without the prior written consent of Goldman Sachs for significant public campaigns: (a) use in advertising, publicity or otherwise the name of Goldman Sachs or any trade name, trademark, trade device, servicemark, symbol or any abbreviation, contraction or simulation thereof owned by Goldman Sachs in a manner that could reasonably harm Goldman Sachs' reputation; or (b) represent, directly or indirectly, that any major product or any service provided by the undersigned has been explicitly approved or endorsed by Goldman Sachs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and subjectivity by adding conditions like 'significant public campaigns,' 'in a manner that could reasonably harm Goldman Sachs' reputation,' and 'explicitly approved or endorsed.' This contradicts the implied strict prohibition in the original text, creating uncertainty about when consent is needed and what constitutes a violation. The contradicted text and changed text are located at the same place. The Change to that location leads to the contradiction.
